background: Gestation is the period of pregnancy from conception to delivery. Uncomplicated human gestations (without intervention) vary according to a Normal distribution with μ=39 weeks and σ=2 weeks.

task.query: According to the '95 part' of the 68-95-99.7 rule, what range of weeks will 95% of human gestations fall into?
task.answer: 95% of human gestations will fall in the range μ ± 2σ = 39 ± 2 × 2 = 39 ± 4 = 35 to 43 weeks.

task.query: What percentage of human gestations will fall outside the range of 35 to 43 weeks?
task.answer: 5% of the values will fall outside of this range.

task.query: What percentage of human gestations will be fewer than 35 weeks, and what percentage will be more than 43 weeks?
task.answer: Since the distribution is symmetrical, 2.5% of gestations will be fewer than 35 weeks (in the left tail of the distribution) and 2.5% will be more than 43 weeks (in the right tail).

meta_info.caption: Tails of the Normal distribution (Gestational length).
